content_body:
If taking advantage of the wide variety of dining options in Blue Ridge, GA suits your fancy, look no further! Though Blue Ridge is known for its outdoor activities, downtown is full of opportunities to refresh and expand your palate. Here's a sample travel itinerary to help you make the most of your culinary adventure:

First Day:

Breakfast at Mountain Mama's Lounge ~ Start your morning off smoothly by heading into downtown for a latte or coffee creation of your choosing at Mountain Mama's! Their syrups and baked goods are all fresh and homemade, so enjoy peaceful ambience and some refreshments before enjoying the rest of your day. Attached to the Blue Ridge Inn, Mountain Mama's Lounge offers a relaxing atmosphere in downtown Blue Ridge to jumpstart your North Georgia adventures.

Lunch at Poke Jon's ~ Visit Poke Jon's for a fresh and exciting poke bowl and enjoy the hip environment while savoring your food. Make sure to visit later in the evening for their inventive cocktails!

Dessert Snack at The Sweet Shoppe ~ For a sweet treat to satisfy your sugar cravings, stop by The Sweet Shoppe for award-winning cupcakes! Winning Cupcake Wars on Food Network, The Sweet Shoppe boasts decadent cupcakes and gourmet desserts paired with some Southern hospitality.

Dinner at Masseria ~ To cap off your foodie adventures for the first day, visit Masseria Kitchen & Bar for homemade pizza and pasta in downtown Blue Ridge. Combining Mediterranean and Appalachian dishes, Masseria welcomes you to partake of some comfort food with a farm-to-table mindset.

Second Day:

Breakfast at The Yellowphant Cafe ~ Serving classic breakfast dishes in a bright atmosphere, The Yellowphant Cafe welcomes you to Blue Ridge! Enjoy some refreshing and hearty meals before experiencing Blue Ridge!

Lunch at Rum Cake Lady ~ Preparing authentic Cuban sandwiches, Rum Cake Lady is a family-owned and operated cafe offering a variety of sandwiches, bowls, and homemade bundt cakes. Their refreshing food is filling and flavorful!

Dinner at Ferraro's Kitchen ~ To add some fine dining to your North Georgia getaway, reserve a table at Ferraro's for a fantastic evening full of fabulous food and conversation. Ferraro's Kitchen boasts outstanding Italian meals and a lovely wine list. Dining at Ferraro's is a must for your Blue Ridge vacation!

Dessert at Mona Lisa's Sweet Shoppe ~ Elevate your evening experience even more with a craft cocktail and delectable sweets. 

Third Day:

Breakfast at Iron Bridge Cafe ~ Spend your last day exploring Aska Rd., sprinkled with memorable hikes, gem mining, tubing, waterfall trails, and more. Start your adventure with a quick breakfast at Iron Bridge! 

Lunch at  Toccoa Riverside Restaurant ~ Take a break and savor a scenic lunch at Toccoa Riverside Restaurant, also located off of Aska Rd. Lunch with a view is always a great idea! 

Dinner at Mystic Mountain Pizza ~ For an easy-going evening, stop by Mystic Mountain Pizza for some homemade gourmet pizza and live music!
title: A Foodie's Weekend Paradise: Restaurants in Blue Ridge, Georgia
